title: Abdominal examination — palpable liver
candidateInstructions: You are an FY1 in the hepatology clinic. Mr Brian Cooper, a 58-year-old man, has been referred by his GP with abnormal liver function tests and right upper quadrant fullness. Please perform an abdominal examination and present your findings.
examinerNotes: Hepatomegaly due to alcohol-related liver disease or hepatic metastases. Examination findings: hepatomegaly 4cm below costal margin, smooth, non-tender. Signs of chronic liver disease (spider naevi, palmar erythema, leukonychia, jaundice). Candidate should comment on these peripheral signs and assess for ascites.

patient.name: Brian Cooper
patient.presentingComplaint: Abnormal liver tests, right upper quadrant fullness

hpi.onset: Gradual, noticed over last few months

alcohol.pattern: Heavy daily drinker — 4-6 cans lager per day for 20 years

systemsReview.dermatological: Spider naevi on chest and back (patient noticed)

examination.general: Mildly icteric, palmar erythema, leukonychia, spider naevi x5 on chest and arms, gynaecomastia
examination.abdominal: Hepatomegaly — liver palpable 4cm below right costal margin, smooth edge, non-tender. Mild splenomegaly. No ascites. No caput medusae. Shifting dullness negative.

investigations.name: Liver Function Tests
investigations.interpretation: Marked hepatitic picture with hyperbilirubinaemia and hypoalbuminaemia. AST:ALT ratio >1.5 suggests alcohol-related liver disease. GGT markedly elevated — typical of alcohol use.

investigations.name: Abdominal Ultrasound
investigations.result: Enlarged liver 18cm with coarsened echogenicity and nodular surface. Mild splenomegaly 14cm. No focal liver lesions. No ascites. No biliary dilatation. Portal vein diameter 14mm (upper limit).
investigations.interpretation: Findings consistent with alcoholic cirrhosis. No focal lesions. Mild portal hypertension.

patient.emotionalState: Defensive about alcohol. Cooperative for examination but reluctant to answer detailed questions about drinking.
patient.communicationStyle: Answers briefly. Minimises alcohol intake when asked.
hiddenConcerns: Am I going to need a liver transplant?
My wife has been telling me to stop for years
doNotVolunteer: Actual alcohol intake — will say 'social drinker' unless pressed with specific questions about daily units
Duration of heavy drinking — only if specifically asked for how long

dataGathering.criterion: General inspection — peripheral signs of liver disease
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Jaundice, spider naevi, palmar erythema, leukonychia, gynaecomastia
Starts from RIF, moves towards costal margin on inspiration
States extent of hepatomegaly in centimetres

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: States palpable liver with size below costal margin

clinicalManagement.criterion: Links findings to alcohol-related liver disease
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Names ALD or cirrhosis as likely cause

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Sensitive approach when discussing alcohol history

references.source: BSG Guidelines on Management of Alcohol-Related Liver Disease 2020

learningObjectives: Identify peripheral stigmata of chronic liver disease
Assess for ascites using shifting dullness
